headline: Rubbish on the beach? Blame the Council!
subtext: Ratepayers should not have to foot the bill for the carelessness of the Council and its contractors.
body:
Waverley, Northern Beaches - The Steyne's footpath is in disarray, as is the public opinion regarding the local council. The Corso has become the epicentre of controversy; according to the B-Line, beachgoers are up in arms about the increasing amount of rubbish they're finding on the sand. 

Council Chambers are under siege by disgruntled locals - and rightly so, considering that it's been a month since their last waste collection service. It seems as though the council's current priority is to organise events rather than managing their beachside duties. 

"Where have all the trash bags gone? asks a bewildered local ratepayer, who remains anonymous for fear of retribution from 'Cr' (councilor) Shirely. "Our beaches used to be pristine; now they're covered in tinfoil and half-eaten pies! It's a travesty!"

The council's response has been predictably inadequate - blaming the beach litter on pesky seagulls who have suddenly developed a taste for discarded fish and chips wrappers.

But here's hoping that common sense will prevail, and our beloved beaches will be returned to their former glory sooner than later.

professor.title: From The Archives
professor.content: The archives are currently closed due to a flea infestation.

classifieds: For Sale: Evil-Looking Gnome. Loves to torment beach-goers. Price Negotiable.
LOST & FOUND: Pet Suckerfish. Ate the neighbor's laundry. Contact for retrieval.
SERVICES: Miracle Gardener. Turns sand into fertile soil. Call now for a 'free' consultation.

mystic.prediction: As The Mystic on Northern Beaches Sydney, Aries natives may face challenges concerning local parking and B-Line bus delays; however, their fortunes lie in surfing opportunities and potential gains in real estate transactions.
